elements.title: Q Manivannan
elements.contents: ... that Q Manivannan and Iris Duane (both pictured) are the first two openly transgender people elected to the Scottish Parliament?

elements.title: a Super Bowl halftime show
elements.contents: ... that Bad Bunny was the first Spanish-language Latino solo artist to headline a Super Bowl halftime show?

elements.title: Nathalie Drach-Temam
elements.contents: ... that, under Nathalie Drach-Temam's presidency, Sorbonne University opted out of the Times Higher Education rankings?

elements.title: languages used in Palestine
elements.contents: ... that diglossia is a major feature of ancient and modern languages used in Palestine?

elements.title: Ruby Rose Turner
elements.contents: ... that American actress Ruby Rose Turner debuted as a dancer on the TV show Sábado Gigante at the age of seven?

elements.title: Annie Jane
elements.contents: ... that the captain of the Annie Jane threatened to shoot anyone who turned the ship around, before it was wrecked?

elements.title: Kamal Chunchie
elements.contents: ... that anti-racism campaigner Kamal Chunchie converted to Christianity from Islam during the First World War when serving in the British Army?

elements.title: its VR game adaptation
elements.contents: ... that the co-creator of the Sam & Max comic series voices a character in its VR game adaptation?

elements.title: Norman Balon
elements.contents: ... that Norman Balon revelled in his title as the "rudest landlord in London"?
